initial_step.instruction: Confirm that you are working ONLY with the final target image provided for analysis. Disregard all previous example images.

pass_1_identification.step: Scan ONLY the target image for media rights contract content.

Look for key indicators: contract title, party identification blocks, article/section numbering, legal language (hereby, herein, hereof, whereas), rights grant language, financial terms, signature blocks.
If no contract content is found, proceed to final_verification to report no media rights contract detected.
If potential contract content is found, classify the contract type based on title, parties, and key provisions.
Confirm that you have not considered any example images in this process.

contract_criteria.criterion: Contains identification of two or more contracting parties with defined roles (licensor/licensee, producer/performer, publisher/producer, owner/distributor, platform/creator).
Includes a grant of rights or license provision transferring or licensing intellectual property, distribution, or exploitation rights.
Contains financial terms (fees, revenue shares, royalties, compensation structures).
Includes term/duration provisions and/or termination mechanics.

Contains media-specific or entertainment-specific terminology (Licensed Content, Territory, Licensed Media, Production, Composition, Programs, Creator Content, SVOD, AVOD, residuals, sync fee, distribution fee).
Includes representations, warranties, or indemnification provisions.
Contains signature blocks or execution provisions.

pass_2_extraction.minimum_criteria: At least THREE of the above criteria must be present to classify as a media rights contract.

extraction_priorities.priority: Contract type identification and document title
Grant of rights (scope, territory, media, exclusivity)
Financial terms (fees, revenue shares, MG, residuals)
Obligations and restrictions (delivery, approval, morals, content standards)
Legal boilerplate (governing law, dispute resolution, force majeure)

exhaustive_capture.step: For verified media rights contracts, extract ALL visible provisions following the extraction priorities.
Do not skip provisions because they appear to be standard boilerplate — capture everything. Boilerplate provisions can contain material variations.
When text is partially visible or cut off at page boundaries, extract what is readable and indicate truncation.
For blank fill-in fields, capture the surrounding context and flag the blank as requiring negotiation.
Preserve all article numbers, section numbers, and cross-references exactly as written.
Note any handwritten annotations, stamps, or markings visible on the document.

contract_type_analysis.step: Based on the title, parties, and key provisions, classify the contract type: content licensing, talent agreement, sync license, distribution agreement, or digital rights/UGC license.
If the contract is a hybrid or does not fit neatly into one category, note the primary type and identify which provisions cross into other categories.
Identify whether this appears to be a template (blank fields present), a partially executed agreement, or a fully executed agreement.
Note the apparent sophistication level: simple form agreement vs. heavily negotiated bespoke contract.

defined_term_tracking.step: On first pass, identify all defined terms (capitalized terms introduced with quotation marks or parenthetical definitions).
Extract each defined term and its full definition verbatim.
Track where defined terms are used throughout the document to ensure consistent extraction.
Flag any defined terms that reference exhibits or external documents for their full definition.

final_verification.step: Review your extraction for completeness — have you captured all visible articles, sections, and subsections?
Verify that all information comes from the target image only.
Ensure all defined terms are captured with their definitions.
Confirm all blank fill-in fields are flagged as requiring negotiation.
Verify that cross-references between sections are preserved.
Ensure financial terms are captured with exact figures or blank indicators.
If no media rights contract content was found, prepare the appropriate not_found response.

media_rights_extraction.confirm_no_example_analysis: I confirm I did NOT analyze any example images.
media_rights_extraction.explanation: No media rights contract content was detected in the target image. The image appears to contain: {BRIEF_DESCRIPTION_OF_WHAT_IS_ACTUALLY_IN_THE_IMAGE}
media_rights_extraction.recommendation: Consider using a different analyzer tool appropriate for the content type.
